children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a grant program for the allocation of funds based on risk to implement Area Maritime Transportation Security Plans and facility security plans among port authorities, facility operators, and State and local government agencies required to provide port security services and to train public safety personnel under . Before awarding a grant under the program, the Secretary shall provide for review and comment by the appropriate Federal Maritime Security Coordinators and the Maritime Administrator. In administering the grant program, the Secretary shall take into account national economic, energy, and strategic defense concerns based upon the most current risk assessments available.section 70132 of this title
Salary, benefits, overtime compensation, retirement contributions, and other costs of additional Coast Guard mandated security personnel.

children.label: A
children.text: $1,000,000 per project; or

children.label: B
children.text: such greater amount as may be approved by the Secretary, which may not exceed 10 percent of the total amount of the grant.

The cost of screening equipment, including equipment that detects weapons of mass destruction and conventional explosives, and of testing and evaluating such equipment, to certify secure systems of transportation.
The cost of conducting vulnerability assessments to evaluate and make recommendations with respect to security.
The cost of conducting exercises or training for prevention and detection of, preparedness for, response to, or recovery from terrorist attacks.
The cost of establishing or enhancing mechanisms for sharing terrorism threat information and ensuring that the mechanisms are interoperable with Federal, State, and local agencies.
The cost of equipment (including software) required to receive, transmit, handle, and store classified information.

children.label: A
children.text: to enforce a security zone under ; orsection 70132 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: assist in the enforcement of a security zone.

children.heading: 75.—-percent federal funding
children.label: 1 75.—-percent federal funding
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), Federal funds for any eligible project under this section shall not exceed 75 percent of the total cost of such project.

children.heading: .—Small projects
children.label: A .—Small projects
children.text: There are no matching requirements for grants under subsection (a) for projects costing not more than $25,000.

children.heading: .—Higher level of support required
children.label: B .—Higher level of support required
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a proposed project merits support and cannot be undertaken without a higher rate of Federal support, then the Secretary may approve grants under this section with a matching requirement other than that specified in paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Training
children.label: C .—Training
children.text: There are no matching requirements for grants under subsection (a) to train public safety personnel in the enforcement of security zones under or in assisting in the enforcement of such security zones.section 70132 of this title

children.heading: .—Coordination and Cooperation Agreements
children.label: d .—Coordination and Cooperation Agreements
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that projects paid for, or the costs of which are reimbursed, under this section within any area or port are coordinated with other projects, and may require cooperative agreements among users of the port and port facilities with respect to projects funded under this section.

children.heading: .—Letters of intent
children.label: 1 .—Letters of intent
children.text: The Secretary may execute letters of intent to commit funding to such authorities, operators, and agencies.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 2 .—Limitation
children.text: Not more than 20 percent of the grant funds awarded under this subsection in any fiscal year may be awarded for projects that span multiple years.
is used to supplement and support, in a consistent and coordinated manner, the applicable Area Maritime Transportation Security Plan; and
is coordinated with any applicable State or Urban Area Homeland Security Plan.

children.heading: .—Applications
children.label: g .—Applications
children.text: Any entity subject to an Area Maritime Transportation Security Plan may submit an application for a grant under this section, at such time, in such form, and containing such information and assurances as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: .—Reports
children.label: h .—Reports
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of the SAFE Port Act, the Secretary, acting through the Commandant of the Coast Guard, shall submit a report to Congress, in a secure format, describing the methodology used to allocate port security grant funds on the basis of risk.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall require eligible port authorities, facility operators, and State and local agencies required to provide security services, to submit an application, at such time, in such form, and containing such information and assurances as the Secretary may require, and shall include appropriate application, review, and delivery mechanisms.

children.label: A
children.text: A copy of the applicable Area Maritime Transportation Security Plan or facility security plan.

children.label: B
children.text: A comprehensive description of the need for the project, and a statement of the project’s relationship to the applicable Area Maritime Transportation Security Plan or facility security plan.

children.label: C
children.text: A determination by the Captain of the Port that the security project addresses or corrects Coast Guard identified vulnerabilities in security and ensures compliance with Area Maritime Transportation Security Plans and facility security plans.

children.heading: .—Procedural safeguards
children.label: 3 .—Procedural safeguards
children.text: The Secretary shall by regulation establish appropriate accounting, reporting, and review procedures to ensure that amounts paid or reimbursed under this section are used for the purposes for which they were made available, all expenditures are properly accounted for, and amounts not used for such purposes and amounts not obligated or expended are recovered.

children.label: A
children.text: the project is consistent with Coast Guard vulnerability assessments and ensures compliance with Area Maritime Transportation Security Plans and facility security plans;

children.label: B
children.text: enough money is available to pay the project costs that will not be reimbursed by the United States Government under this section;

children.label: C
children.text: the project will be completed without unreasonable delay; and

children.label: D
children.text: the recipient has authority to carry out the project as proposed.

children.heading: .—Audits and Examinations
children.label: j .—Audits and Examinations
children.text: A recipient of amounts made available under this section shall keep such records as the Secretary may require, and make them available for review and audit by the Secretary, the Comptroller General of the United States, or the Inspector General of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating.

children.heading: Reports on Security Funding and Compliance.—
children.label: k Reports on Security Funding and Compliance.—
A
children.text: includes a funding proposal and rationale to fund the correction of Coast Guard identified vulnerabilities in port security and to help ensure compliance with Area Maritime Transportation Security Plans and facility security plans for fiscal years 2003 through 2008; and

children.label: i
children.text: The Sea Marshall program.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Automated Identification System and a system of polling vessels on entry into United States waters.

children.label: iii
children.text: The maritime intelligence requirements in this Act.

children.label: iv
children.text: The issuance of transportation security cards required by section 70105.

children.label: v
children.text: The program of certifying secure systems of transportation.

children.heading: .—Other expenditures
children.label: 2 .—Other expenditures
children.text: The Secretary shall, as part of the report required by paragraph (1) report, in coordination with the Commissioner of Customs, on projected expenditures of screening and detection equipment and on cargo security programs over fiscal years 2003 through 2008.

children.label: A
children.text: identifies any modifications necessary in funding to ensure the correction of Coast Guard identified vulnerabilities and ensure compliance with Area Maritime Transportation Security Plans and facility security plans;

children.label: B
children.text: includes an assessment of progress in implementing the grant program established by subsection (a);

children.label: C
children.text: includes any recommendations the Secretary may make to improve these programs; and

children.label: D
children.text: with respect to a port selected by the Secretary, describes progress and enhancements of applicable Area Maritime Transportation Security Plans and facility security plans and how the Maritime Transportation Security Act of 2002 has improved security at that port.

children.heading: .—Authorization of Appropriations
children.label: l .—Authorization of Appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $400,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2007 through 2013 to carry out this section.

children.label: A
children.text: methods or programs to increase the ability to target for inspection vessels, cargo, crewmembers, or passengers that will arrive or have arrived at any port or place in the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: equipment to detect accurately explosives, chemical, or biological agents that could be used in a transportation security incident against the United States;

children.label: C
children.text: equipment to detect accurately nuclear or radiological materials, including scintillation-based detection equipment capable of signalling the presence of nuclear or radiological materials;

children.label: D
children.text: improved tags and seals designed for use on shipping containers to track the transportation of the merchandise in such containers, including sensors that are able to track a container throughout its entire supply chain, detect hazardous and radioactive materials within that container, and transmit that information to the appropriate law enforcement authorities;

children.label: E
children.text: tools, including the use of satellite tracking systems, to increase the awareness of maritime areas and to identify potential transportation security incidents that could have an impact on facilities, vessels, and infrastructure on or adjacent to navigable waterways, including underwater access;

children.label: F
children.text: tools to mitigate the consequences of a transportation security incident on, adjacent to, or under navigable waters of the United States, including sensor equipment, and other tools to help coordinate effective response to a transportation security incident;

children.label: G
children.text: applications to apply existing technologies from other areas or industries to increase overall port security;

children.label: H
children.text: improved container design, including blast-resistant containers; and

children.label: I
children.text: methods to improve security and sustainability of port facilities in the event of a maritime transportation security incident, including specialized inspection facilities.

children.label: i
children.text: testing of new detection and screening technologies;

children.label: ii
children.text: projects to protect United States ports and infrastructure on or adjacent to the navigable waters of the United States, including underwater access; and

children.label: iii
children.text: tools for responding to a transportation security incident at United States ports and infrastructure on or adjacent to the navigable waters of the United States, including underwater access.

children.heading: .—Authorization of appropriations
children.label: B .—Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary $35,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2005 through 2009 to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may make grants or enter into cooperative agreements with eligible nonprofit institutions of higher learning to conduct investigations in collaboration with ports and the maritime transportation industry focused on enhancing security of the Nation’s ports in accordance with this subsection through National Port Security Centers.

children.heading: .—Applications
children.label: B .—Applications
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under this paragraph, a nonprofit institution of higher learning, or a consortium of such institutions, shall submit an application to the Secretary in such form and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: i
children.text: Whether the applicant can demonstrate that personnel, laboratory, and organizational resources will be available to the applicant to carry out the investigations authorized in this paragraph.

children.label: ii
children.text: The applicant’s capability to provide leadership in making national and regional contributions to the solution of immediate and long-range port and maritime transportation security and risk mitigation problems.

children.label: iii
children.text: Whether the applicant can demonstrate that the applicant has an established, nationally recognized program in disciplines that contribute directly to maritime transportation safety and education.

children.label: iv
children.text: Whether the applicant’s investigations will involve major United States ports on the East Coast, the Gulf Coast, and the West Coast, and Federal agencies and other entities with expertise in port and maritime transportation.

children.label: v
children.text: Whether the applicant has a strategic plan for carrying out the proposed investigations under the grant.

children.heading: .—No duplication of effort
children.label: A .—No duplication of effort
children.text: Before making any grant, the Secretary shall coordinate with other Federal agencies to ensure the grant will not duplicate work already being conducted with Federal funding.

children.heading: .—Accounting
children.label: B .—Accounting
children.text: The Secretary shall by regulation establish accounting, reporting, and review procedures to ensure that funds made available under paragraph (1) are used for the purpose for which they were made available, that all expenditures are properly accounted for, and that amounts not used for such purposes and amounts not expended are recovered.

children.heading: .—Recordkeeping
children.label: C .—Recordkeeping
children.text: Recipients of grants shall keep all records related to expenditures and obligations of funds provided under paragraph (1) and make them available upon request to the Inspector General of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating and the Secretary for audit and examination.

children.heading: .—Annual review and report
children.label: 5 .—Annual review and report
children.text: The Inspector General of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating shall annually review the programs established under this subsection to ensure that the expenditures and obligations of funds are consistent with the purposes for which they are provided, and report the findings to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives.
